meaning: adj.(中世纪)商业同业公会的
examples: The Hanseatic merchants had great influence on England during their stay there.
It was founded in 255 by the Teutonic Knights and joined the Hanseatic League in 340.
Prussia did not include the Hanseatic League cities, such as Bremen and Hamburg and the others.
He also brought with him the Hanseatic business rectitude that he had learnt in Hamburg.
In drawing such north-south comparisons, there is a danger of falling into stereotypes and romanticising, say, Germany's influence through the old Hanseatic League.
